steps.title: Check current cloudevents setup
steps.description:
Verify your cloudevents version:
CloudEvents is a specification — check your SDK version (e.g., pip show cloudevents)
This feature requires cloudevents as a dependency.

steps.title: Review cloudevents configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant cloudevents configuration:
I was reading through some of the issues that have been closed, namely #378 and #379, so sorry if this is a duplicate issue.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Message Producer Signature
steps.description:
follow the breadcrumbs here: https://github.com/cloudevents/spec/pull/1102 and see if anything in there is something that you'd like to follow-up on. To me the biggest take-away from that chat is that people are open to something in this space, we just need to find the right technical solution.

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "Message Producer Signature" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: follow the breadcrumbs here: https://github.com/cloudevents/spec/pull/1102 and see if anything in there is something that you'd like to follow-up on. To me the biggest take-away from that chat is that people are open to something in this space, we just need to find the right technical solution.

prerequisites.description: CloudEvents is a specification — check your SDK version (e.g., pip show cloudevents)
